title: Adenocalymma elegans
taxonomy: Adenocalymma elegans is classified in the plant kingdom as a member of the family Bignoniaceae. This family, also known as the bignonia family, is a group of flowering plants that are primarily native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Within the Bignoniaceae family, Adenocalymma elegans is further classified as belonging to the genus Adenocalymma. The genus Adenocalymma is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their showy, trumpet-shaped flowers and their ability to thrive in a variety of environments. By classifying Adenocalymma elegans within these categories, botanists are able to better understand the evolutionary relationships between different species of plants and their unique characteristics.
